bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is very low utility for me, and their “cannot move” message is cheap talk unsupported by any concession history.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession from my maximum. The opponent has repeated an all-valueF offer, which is extremely poor for my preferences, and their “cannot move” messages are not supported by any concession behavior, so I will not treat them as binding. I avoid exactly repeating my previous all-valueA bid by conceding only on issueC to valueB, which is a relatively low-cost concession for me.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early (t=0.04) and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low for my preferences rather than showing meaningful concession. Their messages claim a hard authorization limit, but the offer history shows only near-identical valid bids, so I treat that as cheap talk and avoid over-conceding. I changed my prior bid slightly by conceding on issueC while holding firm on the highest-value issueA and other important issues.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession toward my preferences; their repeated “authorization cap” messages are cheap talk and contradicted by continued rigid, very low-utility offers. I will avoid repeating my immediately previous bid while making only a small concession on issueC, where several values are relatively close for me, while holding firm on the higher-value positions for issueA, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid (about 0.76 raw weighted utility, roughly 85% of my maximum possible score) because it is very early at t=0.08 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain highly unfavorable to me, and their authorization claims are cheap talk, but their latest bid shows movement on issueC to valueC, so I keep that while making only a modest additional concession on issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent’s offers remain quite poor for my preferences, with only small and inconsistent movement. Their messages about authorization are cheap talk; the actual offers suggest they favor low values, but they have at least shown issueC:valueC, so I keep that concession while holding firm on my most valuable issues.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong bid for myself while making a modest concession on lower-cost issues. The opponent’s “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk, but their offers show some movement on issueC and issueD; I will accommodate issueC somewhat while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around the early-stage concession range, since t=0.14 is still early and there is no need to drop near the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim hard limits, but their actual bids have shifted on issueA, issueC, and issueD, so I treat those claims as cheap talk and make a modest concession while holding strong on my important issues.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making limited concessions on issues where the opponent has shown some movement, especially issueA and issueC. The opponent’s messages repeatedly claim no flexibility, but their actual offers have changed over time, so I treat the messages as cheap talk and trust the bidding pattern instead. I am holding firm on issueB and issueD because they are valuable to me and the opponent’s offers are still very low-utility from my perspective.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.77 because it is still early at t=0.18, so I should not over-concede yet. The opponent’s repeated “no authority” messages are not credible because their offers have changed over time, but their bids suggest they may value concessions on issueC/issueD, so I am making a small movement on issueC while holding my highest-priority issueA and strong issueB/issueD positions.

I am targeting a utility around 0.77, which is still appropriate at t=0.20 because there is substantial time left and the opponent’s latest bid remains much lower for me despite some movement. The opponent’s repeated “authorization limit” messages are cheap talk and are contradicted by their actual concessions on issueA and issueC, so I will make a measured concession on issueB/issueC while holding firm on the high-value issueA and issueD positions.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.76 because we are only at t=0.22 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small concessions. Their actual bids show some movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD, but they remain especially firm on issueB; I will concede modestly on issueB and issueC while holding my highest-priority issueA and valuable issueD at valueA.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 because it is only t=0.24, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility packages yet. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly and their repeated “authorization limit” messages are contradicted by actual movement, so I treat them as cheap talk while making a moderate concession on issueA and issueB and holding issueD at my best value.

At t=0.26 there is still time, but the opponent has stayed very tough while making small real concessions despite repeated “no authority” messages, so I am targeting a moderate utility bid rather than dropping near their latest offer. I am conceding on issueB and issueC, where the opponent has repeatedly signaled pressure, while holding firm on issueA and issueD because they are high-value to me. This bid avoids invalid values and does not repeat my immediately previous offer.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.50: this is a meaningful concession from my earlier stronger bids, but still well above the opponent’s latest offer value to me. At t=0.28 there is time left, so I am not accepting their very low package; however, their history strongly signals they prefer valueF/valueE on issueB and issueD, so I am conceding those while holding issueA and issueC at acceptable levels.
